gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The ownership mode of a binding (DN-33 §2 / §8.1 Q2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Owned` is the naive default (MEM-4·B0): the binding holds a reference that must be `Dup`'d per"] # [doc = " extra use and `Drop`'d if unused. `Borrowed` is the output of MEM-4 Increment 1's non-escaping"] # [doc = " borrow elision — a borrowed binding is *read without consuming*, so it needs **no** `Dup`/`Drop`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — a two-state tag with no approximation."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq , Hash)] pub enum Mode { # [doc = " The binding owns a reference: extra uses `Dup`, an unused binding `Drop`s (naive default)."] Owned , # [doc = " The binding is borrowed (read-only, non-consuming): no `Dup`/`Drop` (Increment 1 output)."] Borrowed , }
gaps.reason: enum `Mode` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 — every value already copies structurally; DN-128 §6.1) — not emitted as an impl, not a gap

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " One alternative of a flat [`RcNode::Match`] — mirrors `mycelium_core::Alt`."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub enum RcAlt { # [doc = " A constructor arm binding the constructor's fields to `binders` (in declaration order)."] Ctor { # [doc = " The constructor matched."] ctor : CtorRef , # [doc = " The field binders, in declaration order."] binders : Vec < VarId > , # [doc = " The arm body, in scope of `binders`."] body : RcNode , } , # [doc = " A literal arm matching a representation value."] Lit { # [doc = " The literal value to match."] value : Value , # [doc = " The arm body."] body : RcNode , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `RcAlt` has a named-field variant referencing a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An RC-annotated Core IR node (the first-order fragment + `Dup`/`Drop`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Mirrors `mycelium_core::Node` and adds the [`RcNode::Dup`]/[`RcNode::Drop`] reference-counting"] # [doc = " wrappers and the per-binding [`Mode`]. Produced by [`crate::emit`]; checked by"] # [doc = " [`crate::balance`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — a faithful structural mirror; the RC ops are inserted by the emission pass."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub enum RcNode { # [doc = " A constant value (allocates one reference when evaluated)."] Const (Value) , # [doc = " A variable reference — a **consuming use** (move) of one reference of the variable."] Var (VarId) , # [doc = " A **borrowing read** of a variable — non-consuming (the reference count is unchanged; the"] # [doc = " value must be live). Produced by MEM-4 Increment 1 (borrow elision) for reads in"] # [doc = " reader-primitive positions (`Op`/`Swap` arguments) in the immutable value model, where a"] # [doc = " primitive reads its operands and produces a fresh result, retaining nothing."] Borrow (VarId) , # [doc = " A **sole-owner consuming move** — semantically identical to [`RcNode::Var`] (it consumes one"] # [doc = " reference), but **statically proven** to be the unique owner at this point (reference count"] # [doc = " is exactly 1). Produced by MEM-4 Increment 2 (`rc == 1` reuse annotation, DN-33 §6 D-3): the"] # [doc = " runtime `RcCell::drop_ref` `UniqueOwner` branch is guaranteed to fire here, so the value's"] # [doc = " allocation is **FBIP-reuse-eligible** (the freed cell may be recycled by codegen). The static"] # [doc = " claim is **machine-verified** by [`crate::eval`] (it errors if the rc is not 1 here)."] MoveUnique (VarId) , # [doc = " `dup var; body` — increment `var`'s reference count, then evaluate `body`."] Dup { # [doc = " The variable whose reference count is incremented."] var : VarId , # [doc = " The continuation, evaluated after the increment."] body : Box < RcNode > , } , # [doc = " `drop var; body` — decrement `var`'s reference count (reclaim at zero), then evaluate `body`."] Drop { # [doc = " The variable whose reference count is decremented."] var : VarId , # [doc = " The continuation, evaluated after the decrement."] body : Box < RcNode > , } , # [doc = " `let r = body in (drop var; r)` — evaluate `body` to its result, **then** decrement `var`'s"] # [doc = " reference count (reclaim at zero), then yield the result. Distinct from [`RcNode::Drop`]"] # [doc = " (which decrements *before* its body): the borrowed value must stay live **through** its reads"] # [doc = " inside `body`, so its reclamation is placed *after* the body completes. Produced by MEM-4"] # [doc = " Increment 1 to reclaim a fully-borrowed binding once its reads are done."] DropAfter { # [doc = " The variable reclaimed after `body` completes."] var : VarId , # [doc = " The body evaluated (and read from) before the decrement."] body : Box < RcNode > , } , # [doc = " A let binding. `id` is owned within `body`; its `Dup`/`Drop` annotations are placed at the"] # [doc = " top of `body` by the emission pass."] Let { # [doc = " Bound name."] id : VarId , # [doc = " The bound expression."] bound : Box < RcNode > , # [doc = " The body in which `id` is in scope."] body : Box < RcNode > , } , # [doc = " A paradigm-specific primitive application."] Op { # [doc = " The primitive."] prim : Prim , # [doc = " Operands."] args : Vec < RcNode > , } , # [doc = " The representation-changing node (mirrors `Node::Swap`)."] Swap { # [doc = " The value being converted."] src : Box < RcNode > , # [doc = " The target representation."] target : Repr , # [doc = " The policy that chose/justified the swap."] policy : PolicyRef , } , # [doc = " A saturated constructor application."] Construct { # [doc = " The constructor."] ctor : CtorRef , # [doc = " The field expressions, in declaration order."] args : Vec < RcNode > , } , # [doc = " A flat pattern match."] Match { # [doc = " The value being scrutinised."] scrutinee : Box < RcNode > , # [doc = " The alternatives, tried first-match."] alts : Vec < RcAlt > , # [doc = " The catch-all branch."] default : Option < Box < RcNode > > , } , # [doc = " A lambda abstraction with an explicit ownership [`Mode`] on its parameter."] Lam { # [doc = " The bound parameter."] param : VarId , # [doc = " The parameter's ownership mode (`Owned` in the naive emission; `Borrowed` after elision)."] mode : Mode , # [doc = " The body, in scope of `param`."] body : Box < RcNode > , } , # [doc = " Application — apply `func` to `arg`, call-by-value."] App { # [doc = " The function being applied."] func : Box < RcNode > , # [doc = " The argument."] arg : Box < RcNode > , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `RcNode` has a named-field variant referencing a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl RcNode { # [doc = " Wrap `body` in `n` nested `Dup { var }` nodes (innermost is `body`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Used by the emission pass to give an owned binding one reference per extra use."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — produces exactly `n` `Dup` wrappers."] # [must_use] pub fn dup_n (var : & VarId , n : usize , body : RcNode) -> RcNode { let mut acc = body ; for _ in 0 .. n { acc = RcNode :: Dup { var : var . clone () , body : Box :: new (acc) , } ; } acc } # [doc = " Wrap `body` in a single `Drop { var }` node."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — produces exactly one `Drop` wrapper."] # [must_use] pub fn drop_one (var : & VarId , body : RcNode) -> RcNode { RcNode :: Drop { var : var . clone () , body : Box :: new (body) , } } # [doc = " Wrap `body` in a single `DropAfter { var }` node (reclaim `var` *after* `body`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — produces exactly one `DropAfter` wrapper."] # [must_use] pub fn drop_after (var : & VarId , body : RcNode) -> RcNode { RcNode :: DropAfter { var : var . clone () , body : Box :: new (body) , } } }
